item.title: The Crypto Threat Environment 2026
item.description:
Understanding how crypto breaches occur and what separates organizations that contain them from those that suffer catastrophic losses

The post The Crypto Threat Environment 2026 appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: 83% of Crypto Security Leaders Report Cyberattack Exposure as Sygnia Releases Bybit Investigation Findings
item.description:
Sygnia's investigation into the $1.5 billion Bybit hack reveals how North Korea's Lazarus Group exploited human risk, social engineering, and crypto infrastructure weaknesses to execute the largest crypto theft in history.

The post 83% of Crypto Security Leaders Report Cyberattack Exposure as Sygnia Releases Bybit Investigation Findings appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Crypto Security in 2026: Why Transaction Authority Is the Most Important Security Control
item.description:
Most crypto breaches occur when attackers reach transaction authority. Learn how to secure identities, infrastructure, and authorization systems.

The post Crypto Security in 2026: Why Transaction Authority Is the Most Important Security Control appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Why 73% of Organizations Still Aren’t Ready for a Cyberattack
item.description:
Explore lessons from Sygnia's 2026 CISO Survey on Incident Response Readiness.

The post Why 73% of Organizations Still Aren’t Ready for a Cyberattack appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Inside an AI-Assisted Cloud Attack: Familiar Techniques at Unfamiliar Speed
item.description:
Learn how a threat actor used AI to accelerate a cloud attack from initial access to broad compromise in just 72 hours

The post Inside an AI-Assisted Cloud Attack: Familiar Techniques at Unfamiliar Speed appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Live Webinar: Inside an AI-Assisted Cloud Attack
item.description:
Join a practical session based on Sygnia’s investigation of an AI-assisted cloud intrusion that progressed from initial access to broad compromise in just 72 hours.

The post Live Webinar: Inside an AI-Assisted Cloud Attack appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Sygnia Investigation Finds AI Accelerated Attack Enabled Lone Threat Actor to Rapidly Compromise Enterprise Cloud Environment
item.description:
Financially motivated cyberattack reveals agentic AI workflows to accelerate victim reconnaissance, attack tool development, command structuring and environment-specific adaptation.

The post Sygnia Investigation Finds AI Accelerated Attack Enabled Lone Threat Actor to Rapidly Compromise Enterprise Cloud Environment appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Ransomware Incident Response in 2026: What Has Changed and What CISOs Are Still Getting Wrong
item.description:
Learn what has changed in ransomware incident response in 2026, and what CISOs are still getting wrong

The post Ransomware Incident Response in 2026: What Has Changed and What CISOs Are Still Getting Wrong appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: When Technical Controls Work, Attackers Change the Rules: How a Failed Intrusion Became a Data Exposure Incident
item.description:
Discover how a failed intrusion quickly became a data exposure incident after a threat actor pivoted from blocked technical attacks to social engineering, and why layered defenses, rapid response, and operational safeguards are essential when attackers change the rules.

The post When Technical Controls Work, Attackers Change the Rules: How a Failed Intrusion Became a Data Exposure Incident appeared first on Sygnia.
